description_long: This is a robust axe featuring a thick, black metal head with a broad, rectangular blade and a flat poll, suitable for heavy-duty chopping or splitting tasks. The blade is single-edged, with a straight cutting edge and a pronounced, squared-off poll for striking. The handle is made of light-colored wood, slightly curved for ergonomic grip, and appears to be smoothly finished for comfortable handling. The junction between the head and handle is traditional, with the wooden handle inserted through the eye of the axe head and likely wedged for secure attachment. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or branding, emphasizing function over form. This axe would be commonly found in urban garages, maintenance sheds, or used by municipal workers for landscaping and emergency tasks.
description: A heavy-duty axe with a black metal head and a curved wooden handle, designed for chopping or splitting. The blade is broad and rectangular, with a flat poll and a smooth, ergonomic grip.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad, rectangular blade and the curved wooden handle.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the full length of the handle and the thickness of the axe head.
description_view_2: Rear angled view displaying the flat poll and the secure mounting of the head to the handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile emphasizing the straight cutting edge and the ergonomic curve of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle, smooth finish, oval cross-section, ergonomic grip, traditional style.
attribute_blade_design: Broad, rectangular single-edged blade with a straight cutting edge and flat poll, no beard, utilitarian design.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the handle inserted through the eye of the head.
